messages.content: We refunded a customer their full payment. Do we still owe Razorpay its processing fee?

Yes. Clause 3.4 says Razorpay PA fees remain applicable and payable on each Transaction even if you refunded the payment to your customer, whether through normal refund channels or Razorpay’s instant refund service.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 3.4, REFUNDS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You agree that Razorpay PA fees shall always be applicable and payable by You on each Transaction irrespective of whether You have refunded the same to Your customer either through normal channels of refunds or through the instant refund service of Razorpay PA Group Entities (if availed).

known_facts.fact: You refunded the customer their full payment.

messages.content: A customer paid us through UPI, but we never captured the late-authorized payment. What happens to the money?

Under Clause 3.5, for payments that are late authorized but not captured by you, Razorpay PA may initiate an auto-refund to the customer within five (5) days. So the money may be refunded back to the customer.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 3.5, REFUNDS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You acknowledge and agree that for payments that are late authorized but not captured by You, Razorpay PA may initiate auto-refund to the customer within five (5) days.

known_facts.fact: The merchant did not capture the payment.

messages.content: If someone on our team leaks our Razorpay API credentials and fraudulent transactions happen, can we say Razorpay is responsible because the payments ran on its platform?

No. Under Part A, Clause 2.1, you are responsible for keeping Razorpay API credentials and other “Secure Credentials” confidential, and you are responsible for use of the Platform or Services by anyone using those credentials, including unauthorized access and fraudulent transactions resulting from failure to maintain confidentiality. The clause also says you are solely responsible for all activities under your account or using your Secure Credentials, and that Razorpay PA is not liable for such actions. Separately, Part B, Part I, Clause 4.4 states that Razorpay PA is not responsible for liability arising from Fraudulent Transactions.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 2.1, USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You are responsible for maintaining the secrecy of all Your passwords, keys, security codes, login and account information (“Secure Credentials”) associated with Your account with Razorpay PA and API access.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 2.1, USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You will be responsible for the use of the Platform and/ or Services by You or anyone using Your Secure Credentials including but not limited to any unauthorized access, fraudulent transactions, or loss of data resulting from Your failure to maintain the confidentiality of Your Secure Credentials.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 2.1, USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You are solely responsible for all activities that occur under Your account/in using Your Secure Credentials and Razorpay PA shall not be liable for any such change or action performed by using Your Secure Credentials on the Website.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 4.4, FRAUDULENT TRANSACTIONS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You acknowledge that Razorpay PA shall not be responsible for any liability arising in respect of Fraudulent Transactions whether it is an international or a domestic transaction.

known_facts.fact: Someone on the user's team leaked Razorpay API credentials.
Fraudulent transactions happened after the credential leak.
The transactions ran on Razorpay's platform.

messages.content: A customer says their card was used without permission. Can Razorpay freeze our settlement money?

Clause 4.1 says Razorpay PA may suspend settlements during the pendency of inquiries, investigations, and resolution if Razorpay PA is intimated by a Facility Provider that a customer reported an unauthorised debit of the customer’s Payment Instrument. A card is included within “Payment Instrument” under the definitions. To answer your exact situation: was Razorpay PA intimated by a Facility Provider about the customer’s unauthorised-card-use report?

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 4.1, FRAUDULENT TRANSACTIONS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: if Razorpay PA is intimated, by a Facility Provider, that a customer has reported an unauthorised debit of the customer's Payment Instrument (“Fraudulent Transaction”), then in addition to its rights under clause 16 of Part A: General Terms and Conditions, Razorpay PA shall be entitled to suspend settlements to You during the pendency of inquiries, investigations and resolution thereof by the Facility Providers.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 22, DEFINITIONS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: “Payment Instrument” includes credit card, debit card, bank account, prepaid payment instrument or any other instrument issued under Applicable Law, used by a customer to pay the Transaction Amount.

known_facts.fact: A customer says their card was used without permission.
A card is a type of Payment Instrument under the ToS definition.

missing_facts.fact: Whether Razorpay PA was intimated by a Facility Provider that the customer reported an unauthorised debit of the card/payment instrument.
missing_facts.why_it_matters: Clause 4.1 gives Razorpay PA the right to suspend settlements only when Razorpay PA is intimated by a Facility Provider about such an unauthorised debit report.

clarifying_questions.question: Was Razorpay PA intimated by a Facility Provider about the customer’s report of unauthorised card use?

conditional_outcomes.condition_summary: Razorpay PA was intimated by a Facility Provider that the customer reported an unauthorised debit of the card/payment instrument.
conditional_outcomes.outcome: Razorpay PA is entitled to suspend settlements to the merchant during the pendency of inquiries, investigations, and resolution by the Facility Providers.

conditional_outcomes.condition_summary: Razorpay PA was not intimated by a Facility Provider about the customer’s unauthorised debit report.
conditional_outcomes.outcome: Clause 4.1’s stated condition for suspending settlements on this specific Fraudulent Transaction basis is not established from the provided facts.

messages.content: A fraudulent transaction happened and Razorpay already settled the money to us. What happens now?

Under the ToS, because the amount for the Fraudulent Transaction has already been settled to you, any post-settlement dispute is to be resolved under the specified RBI notifications and related RBI guidelines, not fully within the ToS itself. If the Fraudulent Transaction results in a Chargeback, Clause 4.3 says the Chargeback will be resolved under the Chargeback provisions in the Terms; those provisions allow Razorpay PA to recover the Chargeback Amount from future settlements or other funds it holds, and if those are insufficient, by issuing a debit note requiring reimbursement within 7 days. The ToS does not itself specify the full post-settlement fraud-dispute outcome because Clause 4.2 points to external RBI rules.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 4.2, FRAUDULENT TRANSACTIONS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: If the amount in respect of the Fraudulent Transaction has already been settled to You pursuant to these Terms, any dispute arising in relation to the said Fraudulent Transaction, following settlement, shall be resolved in accordance with the RBI's notification DBR.No.Leg.BC.78/09.07.005/2017-18, dated July 6, 2017 read with RBI's notification DBOD. LEG. BC 86/09.07.007/2001-02 dated April 8, 2002, and other notifications, circulars and guidelines issued by the RBI in this regard from time to time.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 4.3, FRAUDULENT TRANSACTIONS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Subject to clause 4.2 above, if the Fraudulent Transaction results in a Chargeback, then such Chargeback shall be resolved in accordance with the provisions set out in the Terms.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 2.3, CHARGEBACKS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Provided however, if the available Transaction Amounts or other funds are insufficient for deduction of the Chargeback Amount, then Razorpay PA is entitled to issue a debit note seeking reimbursement of the Chargeback Amount. You shall reimburse the Chargeback Amount within seven (7) days of receipt of the debit note.

known_facts.fact: The amount for the Fraudulent Transaction has already been settled to the user.

ambiguity_reason.explanation: Clause 4.2 states that post-settlement disputes for Fraudulent Transactions are resolved under specified RBI notifications and other RBI notifications, circulars and guidelines, so the ToS does not itself provide the full substantive outcome.

messages.content: We want to let a new merchant category collect payments through us on Razorpay. Is that allowed?

It depends on what the new merchant category actually sells or provides, and whether this is a material change from what was declared during onboarding. The Terms say your use of the Services must not facilitate prohibited products/services, and Razorpay PA may act where transactions are unlawful or contravene the prohibited list. They also say that if you materially change the type of products/services provided to end customers from what was declared on the onboarding form, you need Razorpay PA's prior written permission for the new or changed type. Please identify the new category and whether it differs from your onboarding declaration.

Source: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 2.14.item005, USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 2.14.item005, USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Your use of Services does not facilitate the offer, sale or purchase of prohibited products and/or services specified under these Terms.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 16.1.item002, SUSPENSION AND TERMINATION
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You facilitate any transaction which is unlawful or in contravention with ‘Prohibited Products and Services' listed below in clause 17 of Part A: General Terms and Conditions.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 16.1.item008, SUSPENSION AND TERMINATION
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You materially change the type of the products/services provided to end customers and as declared on the onboarding form, without obtaining Razorpay PA's prior written permission to use the Services for the new or changed types of services/products

known_facts.fact: The user wants to allow a new merchant category to collect payments through them on Razorpay.

missing_facts.fact: The specific products or services in the new merchant category, and whether the category is a material change from the products/services declared on the onboarding form.
missing_facts.why_it_matters: The Terms prohibit use of Services to facilitate prohibited products/services, and require Razorpay PA's prior written permission for a material change in the type of products/services provided to end customers from what was declared during onboarding.

clarifying_questions.question: What exact products or services will this new merchant category offer, and is this different from the products/services declared on your Razorpay onboarding form?

conditional_outcomes.condition_summary: The new category involves prohibited products or services under the Terms.

applies_when.mf_001: The new category's products/services fall within prohibited products and/or services specified under the Terms.

conditional_outcomes.outcome: Not allowed under the cited Terms provisions.

conditional_outcomes.condition_summary: The new category is a material change from what was declared on the onboarding form.

applies_when.mf_001: The user materially changes the type of products/services provided to end customers from what was declared during onboarding.

conditional_outcomes.outcome: You need Razorpay PA's prior written permission to use the Services for the new or changed products/services.

conditional_outcomes.condition_summary: The new category is not prohibited and is not a material change from the onboarding declaration.

applies_when.mf_001: The category does not involve prohibited products/services and does not materially change the products/services declared on the onboarding form.

conditional_outcomes.outcome: The cited candidate clauses do not identify a prohibition triggered by those facts.

messages.content: We are launching a real-money fantasy sports feature, but we think it is skill-based. Can we use Razorpay to collect entry fees?

No. Based on the facts stated, Razorpay’s Terms prohibit using the Services or Platform for acceptance of payments related to any form of real money online gaming. Clause 14.10 says you must not use Razorpay for the “sale, promotion, facilitation, or acceptance of payments related to any form of real money online gaming.” The Terms do not carve out an exception merely because the game is claimed to be skill-based.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 14.10, ADDITIONAL TERMS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You expressly covenant and warrant that You shall not use the Services or the Platform for the sale, promotion, facilitation, or acceptance of payments related to any form of real money online gaming or an online money game/online money game service as defined under the Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming Act, 2025.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 17.14, PROHIBITED PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Gaming/gambling which includes lottery tickets, sports bets, memberships/ enrolment in online gambling sites, and related content;

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 2.14.item005, USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Your use of Services does not facilitate the offer, sale or purchase of prohibited products and/or services specified under these Terms.

known_facts.fact: The merchant is launching a real-money fantasy sports feature.
The merchant wants to use Razorpay to collect entry fees for that feature.
The merchant believes the feature is skill-based.

messages.content: Razorpay suspended our account under Clause 16. How long can it hold our settlement funds?

The supplied ToS does not state a fixed maximum period for holding settlement funds after a Clause 16 suspension. Part A, Clause 16.1 says Razorpay PA may immediately suspend the Services and settlement of monies/payments in listed events, but it does not specify how long the suspension of settlement can continue. Separately, if the issue is failure to provide requested KYC documents, Part A, Clause 2.8 says settlement may be stopped until the KYC documents are submitted to Razorpay PA’s sole satisfaction. The ToS also gives Razorpay PA discretion to withhold settlement where it determines settlement is not feasible or suitable, with prior notice before refunding to the source account, but no fixed holding timeline is stated there either.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 16.1, SUSPENSION AND TERMINATION
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Razorpay PA shall have the right to immediately suspend Services and settlement of any monies or payments to You, without any liability to You

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 2.8, USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Your failure to submit the KYC Documents when requisitioned shall entitle Razorpay PA to suspend the Services and/or stop settlement of monies (as applicable) until You submit such KYC Documents to the sole satisfaction of Razorpay PA.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 1.16, PAYMENT PROCESSING
source_clauses.relevant_quote: Razorpay PA reserves the right to withhold such settlement and after giving prior notice to you, shall refund the said amount back to the source account from which it was received.

known_facts.fact: Razorpay suspended the user's account under Clause 16.
The user is asking how long Razorpay can hold settlement funds.

ambiguity_reason.explanation: Clause 16.1 gives Razorpay PA the right to immediately suspend settlement of monies/payments, but the supplied ToS provisions do not specify a maximum duration for holding settlement funds during a Clause 16 suspension. Other cited provisions either make settlement subject to risk/compliance checks or Razorpay PA discretion, or provide a KYC-specific condition, but do not provide a general fixed hold period for Clause 16 suspensions.

messages.content: What exact fraud threshold triggers NPCI-related consequences for us under the Razorpay terms?

The Razorpay Terms do not state an exact numeric fraud threshold. They say you are liable if you breach the fraud amount thresholds provided under the NPCI guideline on “Fraud liability guidelines on UPI transactions” NPCI/2022-23/RMD/001, and that the decision of NPCI or the concerned acquiring bank is final and binding. So the exact trigger depends on the external NPCI guideline, not a threshold specified in the ToS. See Part B, Part I, Clause 4.5.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 4.5, FRAUDULENT TRANSACTIONS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You shall be liable in the event of breach of the fraud amount thresholds as provided under the NPCI guideline on ‘Fraud liability guidelines on UPI transactions' NPCI/2022- 23/RMD/001.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 4.5, FRAUDULENT TRANSACTIONS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: You hereby understand and agree that the decision of the NPCI or the concerned acquiring bank, as the case may be, shall be final and binding.

known_facts.fact: The user is asking about the exact fraud threshold for NPCI-related consequences under the Razorpay terms.

ambiguity_reason.explanation: The ToS refers to fraud amount thresholds in an external NPCI guideline but does not reproduce or define the exact threshold in the supplied terms text.
